codeReview.summary: The export client covers the basic directory layout and pagination mechanics, but it does not fully meet the feature contract for preserving a complete team-scoped export shape. Team-scoped exports still write workspace-global labels, which leaks unrelated-team data into the export artifacts.

issues.description: `filterExportDataToTeamScope` returns `labels: data.labels` instead of filtering labels down to those actually referenced by the selected team's issues. The feature contract explicitly requires team-scoped export artifacts to contain no unrelated team data, but `involute export --team <key>` will still write every workspace label to `labels.json` via `packages/cli/src/commands/export.ts:62-90`, including labels never used by the exported team.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The initial export client included extra source fields (`WorkflowState.type/position`, `Label.color`, `User.displayName/active`, `Issue.priority`) that are not representable in the current Prisma schema or consumed by the import pipeline, so they are silently dropped on import.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Export types/query include these fields in `packages/cli/src/linear/types.ts` and `packages/cli/src/linear/queries.ts`, while the Prisma schema has no matching columns in `packages/server/prisma/schema.prisma` (WorkflowState at line 21, IssueLabel at line 32, User at line 38, Issue at line 46) and `packages/server/src/import-pipeline.ts` only preserves a subset of the exported fields.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, architecture docs, and commit `7d968d132d06dc8b265ea93db086bc5746f9c912`. Found one blocking correctness issue: team-scoped exports still write global labels to `labels.json`, so the exported directory can contain unrelated-team data.
